paragraphs.text:
Strata & Sediment gathers twelve large-format works produced in the gallery's
      basement press between September 2023 and February 2024. Each piece interrogates
      the slow accretion of mark on paper — the way a single sheet, run through the
      drum seven, ten, sometimes fourteen times, accumulates a history that no screen
      can flatten. The works are listed below in the order they appear in the south
      gallery; price reflects edition number and condition. NFS denotes works on
      loan from private collections.
Every sheet here has been pressed against a roller until it remembered something it didn't know it knew.

Editioned works may be reserved in the gallery or via the contact details listed
      on the visit page. Prices are exclusive of framing; Obsidian Fold maintains a
      list of three recommended framers in the Katendrecht district who specialise in
      conservation-grade mounting for large-format prints. Works marked NFS are on
      loan and will return to their lenders at the close of the exhibition on 28 June.

All editions are signed, numbered, and embossed with the Obsidian Fold chop.
      Certificates of authenticity accompany every sale. — ref. 2024.03.OF

A risograph is a stencil duplicator developed in Japan in 1986: ink is forced
      through a wax-coated master onto paper at low pressure, one colour at a time.
      The drum holds soy-based ink in cartridges — burnt sienna, federal blue,
      fluorescent pink — and each layer must dry, often overnight, before the sheet
      returns to the machine for the next pass.

The process is famously imprecise. Registration drifts by a half-millimetre;
      ink density wavers; the rollers leave a faint, satisfying grit. We do not
      correct these things. They are the medium speaking back. Our editions
      typically run between 12 and 60, hand-trimmed on a 1962 Krause guillotine,
      and signed verso in soft graphite.

Catalogue Vol. III, pp. 14–88. All works printed on Zerkall mould-made, 250gsm.
